other_observers: Doug Faulkner, Larry Semo, Glenn Walbek; First found by Pete Plage; also seen by Loch Kilpatrick and others earlier on the 5th
description: A gull smaller and darker-mantled than neighboring Ring-billed Gulls (RBGU) swimming on lake. The rounded, white head had relatively extensive brown blotching with a particular concentration of streaking on the nape. The bill was short, thin, entirely yellow, and appeared to lack a pronounced gonydeal angle. The eyes were dark. Except for some brown on the neck, the underparts were unmarked white; the tail was white. The mantle was a medium gray, obviously darker than that of the RBGUs. The bird had large and well-defined white scapular and tertial crescents and a large white mirror on P10  We never saw the bird get off the water, so cannot describe the wingtip pattern (other than that already mentioned) or leg color.Behaviors: Swimming and, briefly, preening on the water's surfaceCall: none heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
